reasoning: Beto O'Rourke is actively holding town halls in Texas (via his group Powered by People) and recently teased that he would run for the Senate 'if Texans want him to'. However, the July 1, 2025 deadline constraint significantly depresses the probability of a Yes resolution. On May 1, Texas Monthly quoted O'Rourke saying it was 'too early to make a definitive proclamation about 2026 or 2028.' Additionally, O'Rourke benefits from delaying a formal announcement: remaining a non-candidate allows him to raise funds for his PAC without strict FEC limits, and his near-universal name ID in Texas means he doesn't need a year-and-a-half runway (for his 2022 gubernatorial bid, he waited until November 2021 to declare). While he remains a likely contender for the race, the odds of an official announcement in the very brief ~5-week window before July 1 are low.
